text: 71-year-old Geoff Dornan hasn't run into trouble with the law, he's rollerbladed into it.
Geoff of Normanhurst, Ormskirk has been weaving in and out of bollards and bylaws in Southport's Chapel Street on his beloved rollerblades since 2002.
But Sefton Council say that his behaviour is a danger and an annoyance, so they're using the Good Rule in Government Act 2005 to try to prosecute the OAP.
In North Sefton Magistrates Court yesterday, Geoff sought to prove his skating was safe and claimed that he always puts pedestrians first.
I feel as if I've done nothing wrong, so I'm very content that it should be looked at carefully.
And I'm rather hoping, of course, that the court comes to my view of this, namely that I'm not doing any harm.
But Geoff's battle for his right to rollerblade splits opinion amongst Southport shoppers.
I think he's a menace. He skates along here when there's prams and elderly people.
He should be stopped and three cheers to the council for trying to stop him.
I think you should let him skate on. He's been a nice guy.
He seems like a nice guy. And what do you reckon, that he's 71 and he's still skating?
Active body, active mind, I say.
The people I'm concerned about is Southporters, a lot of older people here and sometimes when he goes through on his skates, some old people.
My wife's mother is 92 and she walks around here and if he runs into her, it could probably kill her.
I think it's fantastic to see a man like Geoff.
A man of his age. I mean, I can't even skate myself and it's quite inspiring to see someone like him.
And it just brings a smile to everybody's face.
But Geoff will have to wait until February 24th to find out his skating fate.
